description: Inside a moving vehicle, a male voice off-screen says, "Use them wisely." Joey is asleep in the passenger seat with his head tilted back against the window. Phoebe is driving, looking forward.
The sound of the car engine and road noise is audible. Phoebe glances toward the passenger seat, then turns her gaze back toward the road ahead.

Phoebe continues to look between the road and Joey, who remains asleep with his mouth slightly open. "Come on, Joey. You can't win if you don't ask any questions," she says. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey suddenly snaps his eyes wide and lets out a loud shout, his hands rising toward his face. Phoebe flinches and looks at him. Audience laughter continues.

Joey looks around the car interior as Phoebe says, "What? Well, you promised me a fun road trip. And we've been on the road for 6 hours and you've been asleep for five and a half. We" Phoebe keeps her gaze primarily forward as she speaks.

"are switching at the next rest stop and you are going to drive the whole way back. That will be your punishment, you greedy sleeper," Phoebe says, turning her head toward Joey. Joey's mouth is closed and he looks toward the windshield.
Joey nods his head slowly and looks down away from Phoebe.
Joey looks back toward Phoebe and says, "Right. All right. Yeah." Phoebe then adds, "And until then, you are going to sing to me. Because the radio is broken and you are selfish, but"
Phoebe glances briefly at Joey and says, "you have a nice voice." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey looks toward the car dashboard and then turns his head back toward Phoebe.
Phoebe looks straight ahead at the road and says, "Really sing."
Joey takes a breath and prepares to sing, his head tilted slightly.
Joey leans his head back and sings loudly, "Wanna rock and roll all night?" Phoebe smiles and keeps her gaze on the road.
Instrumental theme music with a steady beat begins. The setting changes to an exterior of a two-lane highway where a yellow taxi cab drives away toward a horizon with mountains.
The yellow cab continues driving along the straight road under a clear sky.
Inside the cab, Joey is in the driver's seat with his eyes closed and hands on the wheel. "Man, this is a long drive," his voice is heard as if thinking. Phoebe is asleep in the back seat.
Joey's head remains still. "Are my eyes open?" his voice is heard again.
With his eyes still closed, Joey's voice says, "No." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey's eyes snap open wide. The yellow cab is shown from the exterior driving along the road. The instrumental music continues.
The taxi cab continues its movement along the highway, passing a figure standing on the shoulder of the road.
The taxi cab continues driving along the road.
Inside the cab, Phoebe wakes up in the back seat, blinking and looking around. Her head rises from the seatback. Joey is asleep next to her in the back seat.
Phoebe looks toward the front seat and leans forward. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe gasps and lets out a sharp shout, her hands moving to her chest. A man with thinning hair, Rick, is in the driver's seat and looks back over his shoulder toward her.
Rick says, "Morning." Phoebe's mouth remains open in a gasp. Audience laughter continues.
The cab continues down the road. The setting changes to a view of the New York City skyline with several tall buildings.
Inside a coffee shop, Rachel sits on an orange couch reading a magazine. Ross enters through the door in the background and walks toward her.
Ross walks to the couch and says, "Hey." Rachel looks up from her magazine toward him.
Rachel says, "Hey, hubby." Audience laughter is heard. Ross sits down on the couch next to Rachel, holding a soda can.
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "Yeah, actually, I wanted to talk to you about that whole annulment thing. Yeah, I'm not going to do" Rachel sets her magazine down and looks at him.
Ross finishes his sentence, "that's." He holds the soda can with both hands in front of him. Rachel holds a red mug and stares at Ross.
Both characters remain still in silence as they maintain eye contact. Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel begins to laugh, and Ross joins her, both smiling and looking at each other.
Still laughing, Rachel says, "Okay."
Rachel continues laughing, leaning her head forward.
"So we'll just stay married," Rachel says, her laughter subsiding as she looks at Ross.
Ross responds, "Yes, exactly," with a nod.
Rachel smiles and says, "And I will make everyone call me Mrs."
"Geller," Rachel finishes. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel continues to smile at Ross.
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "Wow. This is so amazing."
Ross keeps his gaze on Rachel, his smile steady.
Ross says, "I really thought I'd have to talk you into this more." He gestures with his right hand. Rachel's smile fades and her expression becomes flat.
Rachel keeps her gaze on Ross, her lips pressed together.
Rachel says, "Okay, see, now I'm scared, because I don't actually think you're kidding." Ross maintains his expression. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel's gaze remains fixed on Ross, her eyebrows slightly drawn together.
Ross says, "I'm not kidding," his volume softer than his previous lines.
Ross continues, "Look," and begins to gesture.
Ross leans slightly toward Rachel.
Ross says, "I can't have three failed marriages. I can't," his volume rising on the second "I can't."
Ross raises his right hand with his fingers spread. "Okay? I am not going to be that guy," he says. Rachel's arms are crossed as she listens.
Ross lowers his hand slightly and looks at Rachel.
Ross continues, "No, that is not going to be my thing." Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel's eyebrows are raised as she continues to look at Ross.
Rachel says, "So will we just stay married forever?" Ross responds, "I don't know."
Rachel's head tilts slightly as she continues watching Ross's face.
Ross says, "I haven't worked it all out yet. But look, okay, I know it's a bit crazy." He moves both hands in front of him. Rachel says, "Yeah."
Ross continues, "Okay. How is this going to affect you, really?"
Rachel's expression remains flat and her arms stay crossed.
Ross says, "I mean, you fill some form out once in a while. Instead of checking the box that says miss,"
"you check the box that says" Ross continues, gesturing with his fingers as if marking a form in the air.
"Mrs. It's right next to it," Ross finishes. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross holds both hands up with his palms facing Rachel. Rachel continues to look at him without speaking.
Rachel and Ross sit across from each other in a coffee shop. Rachel gestures with both hands, palms facing each other about a foot apart.
Rachel says, "Okay, I'm sorry. You're right. You know what? We absolutely can stay married," her hands moving in a small outward circle before resting on her lap. Ross watches her without moving.
Rachel continues, "Because I was under the impression that the boxes were far away from each other," holding her hands up again and then moving them closer together. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross begins to speak, raising his hands slightly from his knees. Rachel looks toward him.
Ross says, "No," while bringing his thumb and forefinger close together in a pinching gesture.
Rachel presses the palm of her hand to her forehead, lowers her gaze, and exhales audibly. Ross lowers his hands and maintains his gaze on her. Audience laughter continues.
Rachel looks at Ross and says, "just please take a moment here and think about what you're asking of me," with her hands held out, palms up.
Ross says, "Okay. I am asking you to do me a favor," his volume steady. He gestures with his right hand toward Rachel and then toward his own chest.
Rachel's hands remain in the air, palms out, as she keeps her gaze fixed on Ross. Silence from both characters.
Rachel says, "You are asking me to be your wife," her volume rising on "wife." Her left hand is raised with the palm toward Ross.
Ross says, "And as my wife, I think you should grant me this favor," his pace slower than his previous line. He points a finger toward Rachel and then brings both hands together.
Rachel lowers her hands to her lap and looks down. Audience laughter is audible. Ross remains still.
Ross says, "We did make vows," while nodding slightly.
The setting changes to a hallway where Monica limps toward a door, leaning on the arm of Chandler. Chandler carries two black bags.
Monica says, "That kid kicked me really hard on the plane," leaning against the wall. Chandler says, "Well, you did pull his hair," while holding the bags.
Monica says, "He took my snack," her volume rising. Chandler looks at her.
Chandler says, "I'm not getting into this again," his volume flat. He turns toward the apartment door.
Chandler stands by the door as Monica continues to lean against the wall, reaching down toward her ankle.
Monica says, "You know what really bad?" Chandler says, "Well, I told you not to walk here," his pace quick. He sets the bags down, bends, and lifts Monica into his arms.
Monica says, "Okay," as Chandler carries her through the open apartment door.
Inside the apartment, Chandler stands in the doorway holding Monica. She has her arms around his neck. They both look forward toward the living room. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler and Monica remain in the same position, looking toward the interior of the apartment without moving.
Monica says, "This hasn't made anything, does it?" Chandler says, "No. Okay," while beginning to step further into the room.
The setting changes to the interior of a car at night. Phoebe sits in the driver's seat of a taxi. Rick sits in the passenger seat and Joey sits in the back. Phoebe looks toward Rick.
Phoebe says, "How could you pick up a hitchhiker?" her volume loud. Rick keeps his gaze ahead.
Phoebe continues to look at Rick while holding the steering wheel. Joey watches them from the back seat.
Phoebe says, "He could be a rapist or a killer or something," gesturing toward Rick with her right hand.
Joey says, "Don't you think I asked him that before he got in?" Phoebe looks back at Joey and says, "You know what?"
Phoebe says, "I'm not talking to you. You go back to sleep. And you. Are you a rapist?" pointing at Rick. Rick turns his head toward her.
Rick says, "No," his volume soft. Phoebe says, "Do you like car games?" looking back at the road.
Rick says, "Yeah. You know, the license plate game?" Phoebe says, "I love the license plate game," her pace fast. Rick turns further toward her.
Joey says, "I'll play. I'll play," his volume louder than Rick's. Phoebe says, "No, you need your sleep. Night night," while shushing him and reaching back to press her hand against Joey's chest.
Audience laughter and upbeat instrumental music with a drum beat are audible. The interior of the car remains visible.
Now outside, a bridge spans across a river with a city skyline visible in the background under a purple and orange sky. The upbeat music continues.
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Ross enters through the front door, wearing a red shirt and jeans. Rachel stands in the room wearing a blue top.
Ross says, "Listen, I know you wanted to talk to me but I have an idea that may make you want to stay married," while walking toward Rachel. Rachel stands with her hands clasped.
Ross stops walking a few feet from Rachel. Rachel keeps her gaze on him.
Ross says, "We register, and you get to keep all the presents," his volume slightly lower than his previous line. He holds his hands out with palms up.
Rachel looks at Ross without speaking or moving. Ross keeps his hands raised. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel says, "No rock. Come on. Listen," while gesturing with her hands for Ross to sit. Ross says, "Listen," simultaneously.
Ross sits on a couch while Rachel sits on the edge of a wooden coffee table directly in front of him.
Rachel says, "Look, I thought a lot about how to tell you this. And actually, I came up with analogy about a little goldfish," her hands clasped in front of her.
Rachel says, "in a pond that freezes over in the winter. But then I realized that was more of a story about what to do when people," gesturing with her fingers as she speaks.
Rachel says, "pick on you. So I'm going to actually save that one for my children's book." Ross says, "Great. Yeah," his volume soft. Rachel claps her hands once.
Ross says, "What's up?" Rachel says, "Oh, sorry. Okay, the bottom line, Ross, is we cannot stay married," with her hands pressed together.
Ross looks at Rachel. Rachel maintains eye contact. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross says, "I don't know if that's true," while pointing a finger at Rachel. Rachel says, "It is," her pace quick.
Rachel keeps her hands pressed together and her gaze on Ross. Ross lowers his hand.
Ross says, "Okay, you know what this is? This is a difference of opinion," moving both hands side to side as if weighing items.
Rachel looks at Ross's hands and then back to his face. Ross continues the hand movement.
Ross says, "And when that happens in a marriage," before Rachel interrupts, "no, Ross, come on. This is not a marriage. This is the world's worst hangover." Rachel stands up.
Rachel stands in front of the seated Ross, her hands by her sides.
In a living room, Rachel stands near a sofa where Ross is seated. She leans slightly toward him.
"Ross, listen, if you do not get this annulment, I will," Rachel says, pointing a finger toward Ross and then toward herself. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross remains seated, his gaze fixed on Rachel as she stands before him. The audience laughter continues briefly before fading.
Ross says, "All right, I'll do it," while looking up at Rachel. She responds, "Thank you," as she stands with her hands at her sides. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross turns his head to the side while Rachel remains standing in front of him. Audience laughter continues.
Rachel raises both hands with palms facing upward and asks, "Is there any such thing as an annulment shower?" Audience laughter is audible.
Ross stands up and walks away. Now outside at night, a yellow taxi drives down a street. Instrumental music with a rhythmic beat plays.
Now inside the taxi, Phoebe is driving while Rick sits in the passenger seat and Joey sits in the back. Rick looks toward Phoebe.
Rick says, "Oh, wait. There's a train station. This is where I get off. Well, I have your address and phone number. And I have your name. And".
Phoebe continues looking at the road while driving and says, "the fact that you're a". Joey watches from the back seat.
Rick keeps his head turned toward Phoebe while she maintains her gaze on the road.
Phoebe says, "well, it's pretty much in your court." Phoebe turns her head slightly toward Rick and smiles.
Rick nods his head once while Phoebe looks back at the road.
Rick says, "All right. See you. Phoebe."
Rick exits the taxi and shuts the door. Phoebe begins driving again. Joey remains in the back seat, looking forward.
Phoebe adjusts her grip on the steering wheel while looking at the road. Joey remains still in the back seat.
Joey leans forward toward the front seat and says, "Come on, Phoebes. I can't take this anymore. Please talk to me."
Phoebe keeps her gaze on the road, her facial expression unchanging. Joey remains leaned forward.
Joey says, "Let me make it up to you," while keeping his gaze on Phoebe.
Joey takes a breath and continues to look at Phoebe.
Joey sings, "Ground control to Major Tom," his volume higher than his previous line. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe continues to drive, her face remaining still while Joey watches her from the back.
Joey sings, "commencing countdown engines on," while leaning closer to the front seat. Audience laughter continues.
Joey places his hands on the back of the front seat and keeps his gaze on Phoebe.
Joey sings, "take your protein pills and put your helmet on," then makes an exhaling sound. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey continues making engine-like sounds while leaning between the front seats. Phoebe's gaze remains forward.
Joey makes another engine sound. Phoebe turns her head slightly toward him and says, "Stop it! Stop it, Joe!"
Phoebe says, "That's not fair. You know I can't resist that beautiful voice," as Joey smiles in the back seat. Audience laughter is heard.
Joey leans further forward, his head near Phoebe's shoulder.
Joey says, "I'm so sorry."
Joey pauses briefly, his gaze remaining on Phoebe.
Joey says, "I know I promised you a fun road trip with your friend and I didn't deliver. Okay?" while moving his hands.
Joey says, "But now I know that you think being awake is an important part of friendship," his hands continuing to move.
Joey shifts his position slightly while still leaning forward and looking at Phoebe.
Joey says, "So I will strive to stay awake for as long as I know you," while Phoebe looks at him briefly in the rearview mirror.
Joey maintains his gaze on Phoebe's reflection in the mirror.
Phoebe says, "can still sleep at night and stuff," and smiles while looking ahead at the road.
Joey says, "Thank you."
Joey leans back and smiles while looking forward. Phoebe continues to drive.
Joey says, "Can we play 20 Questions now? I got a really good one. I've been thinking about it since Kansas."
Phoebe says, "Okay," while continuing to drive and smile.
Phoebe looks at the road, her lips slightly parted as she thinks.
Phoebe asks, "Is it a kind of hot sandwich?" while keeping her gaze on the road.
Joey says, "Yes," his volume softer than his previous response.
Phoebe smiles and nods slightly while driving.
Phoebe asks, "Is it a meatball sub?" Joey responds, "That is incredible." Joey points a finger toward Phoebe. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey says, "You are the master," while still pointing his finger.
Joey and Phoebe both laugh while the taxi drives on.
Rhythmic instrumental music plays. The setting changes to an apartment living room where Rachel stands holding a large bouquet of flowers. Monica and Chandler are also present.
Monica walks across the room and catches the flowers as Rachel throws them. Chandler stands near a kitchen counter, watching. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel wipes her face with her hand. Monica holds the flowers and looks toward Chandler. Audience laughter continues.
Chandler says, "Well, you didn't have to catch them," while looking at Monica.
Monica looks down at the flowers and then back at Chandler.
Rachel says, "It's funny. You look like you're going to be the," and points a finger toward Monica. Audience laughter is heard.
Monica says, "don't say it," while Chandler says, "Don't even think it," their lines overlapping.
Rachel looks between Monica and Chandler while holding her hands near her waist.
Rachel says, "All right. Chandler. Enjoy your handful," and hands the flowers back to Monica before walking away. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica puts the flowers down on a table while Chandler remains standing near the counter with his hands in his pockets.
Chandler says, "All right. Should we just get married? You know, I mean, should we just do it?" with both arms extended outward.
Chandler says, "All the signs are telling us to do it." Monica responds, "I'm sick of the signs. It's too fast. I'm happy the way things are."
Chandler says, "Me too," his volume softer than his previous line.
Monica asks, "I don't want things to change. Do you?" Chandler says, "No." Monica says, "All right, then." Monica sits down on the sofa.
Monica says, "Then nothing changes. Everything is great. Everything stays the same. Now you go unpack because it's been three days and it's driving me insane".
Chandler turns his head and looks toward the side of the room. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "jeez, relax," and begins to walk away.
Monica says, "It's not like we're mad," while Chandler is off-screen.
Monica remains seated on the sofa, her gaze following Chandler.
Inside a living room, a purple door with a yellow frame around the peephole is closed. Laughter is audible. A person’s head briefly appears at the edge of the doorframe from the outside.
The door opens and Chandler, wearing a tan short-sleeved button-down shirt over a white t-shirt, enters. He looks back at the door as he closes it. Audience laughter continues.
Monica, wearing a white long-sleeved shirt, lies on a patterned sofa with her head resting on her hand, looking toward the door.
Chandler stands in the living area, his hands behind his back. He shifts his gaze toward the kitchen area.
Chandler looks toward Monica and says, "I was thinking, what if I..."
Chandler pauses, his gaze remaining on Monica.
Chandler continues, "unpack here?" He stands still.
Monica sits up slightly on the sofa and says, "Then all your stuff would be here."
Chandler stands by the kitchen counter, looking toward Monica. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "Well," his volume slightly lower.
Chandler walks further into the kitchen area, his hands in his pockets.
Chandler stops and says, "what if all my stuff was here?" He looks down briefly then back at Monica.
Monica leans forward, gesturing with one hand, and says, "Then you'd be going back and forth all the time. I mean," her pace is faster than Chandler's previous line.
Monica continues, "doesn't make any sense." She keeps her gaze on him.
Chandler stands in the kitchen, looking toward Monica. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "Okay," his volume flat.
Chandler stands still, his hands in his pockets, looking at Monica.
Chandler says, "what if we live together and you understand what I'm saying," speaking without pauses.
Monica remains on the sofa, her eyes wide as she looks at Chandler.
Monica says, "live together?" her pitch rising at the end.
Monica stands up from the sofa and walks toward Chandler.
Monica stops a few feet from Chandler and says, "There have been no signs for that." Chandler responds, "Me asking is kind of a sign," his volume slightly lower than Monica's.
Monica stands with her hands clasped, looking at Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica smiles, nods her head, and says, "Yes. Okay."
Monica shouts, "Yes!" as she jumps forward, wrapping her arms and legs around Chandler. Chandler holds her up. Audience cheering and applause are foregrounded.
Chandler holds Monica in the kitchen and says, "Yes."
Monica steps down, says, "Okay, wait," and turns toward the kitchen counter.

Monica reaches into a blue bowl, pulls out a key, and hands it to Chandler, saying, "Okay. Here's a key. Okay, here's your key. All right. Now, you have to go out and pretend. Now go out and then come back..."
Monica pushes Chandler toward the purple door.
Chandler stands by the door, holding the key, and says, "in. Door hasn't been locked in five years, but okay."
Chandler exits and Monica closes the door. She stands with her hands clasped near her face.
Chandler (off-screen) asks, "Ready?"
Monica leans toward the door and says, "Ready!" her volume loud.
A metallic snapping sound is audible. Chandler (off-screen) says, "Okay, a little problem. The key broke in the lock, and I can't get in." Monica presses her ear to the door.
Monica tries to turn the doorknob, but it does not move.
Monica says, "This isn't a sign?" Chandler (off-screen) responds, "No, it's not a sign. It's a very old key. It's an old key. Oh, my God. It's old." Monica steps back from the door.
Monica leans back against the doorframe.
Chandler (off-screen) says, "I love you."
Monica looks toward the door.
Monica says, "I love you," her volume softer than the previous line.
Monica leans her head against the purple door.
Chandler (off-screen) asks, "Are you hugging the door right now?"
Monica remains leaning against the door, her eyes looking forward. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica's gaze shifts slightly to the side while she keeps her head against the door.
Monica says, "No," her voice slightly strained.
Monica keeps her head against the door.
Chandler (off-screen) says, "Yeah, me neither."
Instrumental music with a rhythmic beat starts.
Inside a coffee shop, Rachel and Phoebe sit on an orange sofa. Ross walks toward them from the background. Rachel wears a light blue tank top, and Phoebe wears a green long-sleeved shirt.
Ross says, "Hi," as he approaches the sofa.
Rachel and Phoebe look up at Ross.
Rachel stands and asks, "Hey. So did everything go all right with the annulment?" Ross pauses and then responds, "Oh, yeah," while nodding.
Ross continues, "No problems. It's all taken care of." He gestures with his hands. Rachel says, "Ross thank you."
Rachel wraps her arms around Ross, and they hug. Phoebe, holding a pink book, watches them. Audience "aww" sound is audible.

Rachel pulls back and says, "Hey, you guys want to go see a movie?" Ross says, "Oh, yeah. Why not? Yeah." Rachel looks at Phoebe and asks, "Pheebs?" Phoebe looks up from her book and says, "No, thanks, I've already seen one."
Rachel and Ross look at each other. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel says, "Hey, I'm going to get my sweater," and walks toward the back of the shop. Ross says, "Okay."
Ross sits on the sofa next to Phoebe. He looks around then leans toward her.
Ross asks, "You want to hear something weird?"
Phoebe looks at him and says, "Always."
Ross looks toward the back of the room, then back at Phoebe.
Ross says, "I didn't get the annulment," his volume lower.
Phoebe stops moving her hands. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe says, "What?" her pitch high.
Ross leans closer and says, "We're still married."
Phoebe looks forward, her mouth slightly open.
Ross says, "Don't tell Rachel. See you later," then stands up and walks away.
Phoebe remains seated, looking ahead with a still expression. Audience laughter is audible.
The instrumental music continues as white text appears on a black background, reading "FOR COURTENEY AND DAVID WHO DID GET MARRIED."
More credits appear on the black background as the music continues.
The scene changes to a hallway outside an apartment with a green door numbered "20". Chandler leans against the wall, working on the lock with a tool. Joey walks down the stairs and approaches.
Joey asks, "What are you doing?" Chandler responds, "Keys stuck in the lock," without looking up. Joey says, "I can fix it. Hold on."
Joey walks into the apartment next door. A sawing sound begins.
Joey exits the neighboring apartment holding a large handsaw and says, "Watch out, watch out," as he moves toward Chandler.
Joey kneels in front of the door and begins sawing the lock. Chandler stands behind him, looking down.
Joey stands up, puts the saw behind his back, and gestures toward the door.
Chandler tries the doorknob, which still does not move. He says, "It still doesn't work." Joey says, "I'm not finished."
Joey kneels back down and begins sawing into the wood of the door frame.
Chandler looks at Joey as he continues sawing. The sound of the saw against wood is foregrounded.

Inside an apartment, Joey, wearing a white t-shirt, and Chandler, in a beige button-down shirt, stand in a doorway near a green wooden unit on the floor. Monica stands by a kitchen counter. Loud audience laughter and cheering are audible.
Monica looks down at the green unit while resting her hand on the counter. Joey turns his head toward Chandler and smiles. The audience laughter gradually subsides.
Chandler stands with his hands behind his back, looks toward Joey, and says, "nice job, Joe."
Chandler says, "You're quite the craftsman," his pitch rising on the final word. Joey's gaze remains on the green unit.
Joey steps over the green unit, creating a thumping sound on the wood, and walks through the doorway into the hallway. Monica watches him move.

Chandler turns and walks out the doorway behind Joey. Monica briefly raises her right hand and looks toward them. An upbeat, bass-heavy instrumental music track begins and on-screen text credits appear.
The scene of the apartment kitchen and the green unit remains visible as white text credits roll over the image. The instrumental music continues at a steady volume.
The screen goes black. The instrumental music continues for several seconds before fading to silence at the end of the segment.
